_description: All math UEPG concepts at grade 4 and below (grades K–4): counting, place value, basic operations, introduction to fractions, basic geometry

uepgs.title: Averages
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: mean and its applications — computing average, finding a missing value, adjusting for added/removed values, weighted average of groups, overlapping-segment trick, and average-speed trap
uepgs.skills_summary: compute average (sum÷count), find missing (total−known), add/remove one value (new sum÷new count), weighted average (n₁A₁+n₂A₂)÷(n₁+n₂), overlap element (Sum₁+Sum₂−Total), average speed trap 2v₁v₂÷(v₁+v₂)

uepgs.title: From Words to Multiple Equations
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: setting up a system of equations from a word description — sum & difference, count + quantity (chicken-rabbit), two price scenarios, ratio + total, and three-variable systems
uepgs.skills_summary: sum & difference (x+y=a, x-y=b), count+quantity (x+y=H, ax+by=Q), two price scenarios (a1x+b1y=c1, a2x+b2y=c2), ratio+total (x=ry, x+y=T), three variables (three equations), build & verify

uepgs.title: From Words to One Equation
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: translating word descriptions into algebraic equations — direct language, reversed phrasing, two-step (ax+b=c), parentheses (a(x+b)=c), multi-sentence stories, and choosing x wisely
uepgs.skills_summary: direct translation (x+a=b x-a=b ax=b x÷a=b), reversed phrasing (a-x=b times-as-many fraction-of more-than), two-step setup (ax+b=c), parentheses setup (a(x+b)=c), real-world stories, choose x and check

uepgs.title: Fraction Basics
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: introduction to fractions — what a fraction means, reading and writing unit and non-unit fractions, comparing fractions with the same numerator or denominator
uepgs.skills_summary: parts of a whole, unit fractions (1/2 1/3 1/4 1/6 1/8), non-unit fractions, comparing with same denominator, comparing with same numerator, fraction misconceptions

uepgs.title: Fraction Simplification
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: reducing a fraction to lowest terms by dividing numerator and denominator by their greatest common divisor (GCD)
uepgs.skills_summary: basic reduction by GCD, simplification within fraction division, parentheses-first operations, variable factor cancellation, nested fraction simplification

uepgs.title: Smart Whole Number Arithmetic
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: calculation strategies for whole number expressions — common factor, complement to round number, two-level regrouping, ratio shortcut, and factor splitting (125×8=1000, 25×4=100)
uepgs.skills_summary: common factor (distributive law), complement to round number (99×n, 98×n), two-level regrouping, ratio shortcut between whole numbers, factor splitting (friendly pairs), strategy selection

uepgs.title: Chicken-Rabbit Problems
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: solving two-unknown problems (chicken-rabbit style) using the assumption method — guess-and-adjust, assume all small, assume all large, leg-raising visualization, and three-type reduction
uepgs.skills_summary: guess and adjust, assume all small type, assume all large type, leg-raising visualization, three-type reduction to two-type, strategy selection

uepgs.title: Linear Equation in One Variable
uepgs.subject: arithmetic
uepgs.concept_fingerprint: solving one-variable linear equations using the balance model and inverse operations — x+a=b, x-a=b, ax=b, x÷a=b, two-step ax+b=c and a(x+b)=c, word problem setup, solution verification
uepgs.skills_summary: equation concepts and balance model, one-step add/subtract (x+a=b x-a=b a-x=b), one-step mul/div (ax=b x÷a=b), two-step (ax+b=c and a(x+b)=c), word problem setup (6 templates including chicken-rabbit bridge), verify and reflect
